title: Vinyl Siding Replacement

budget.description: Economy-grade vinyl (.040 thickness), basic colors, standard installation on a 1,500 sq ft exterior, no insulation backing

midRange.description: Mid-grade vinyl (.044 thickness), wider color range, insulated backing, new J-channel and trim on a 1,500 sq ft exterior

premium.description: Premium insulated vinyl (.046+ thickness), shake or board-and-batten profiles, architectural trim, house wrap replacement on a 1,500 sq ft exterior

costFactors.title: Siding Grade and Thickness
costFactors.description: Vinyl siding ranges from .040 economy panels ($2-$3/sq ft installed) to .046+ premium insulated panels ($7-$12/sq ft installed). Thicker panels resist impact damage better, look more realistic, and last longer. The jump from economy to mid-grade (.044) adds about $1.50-$2 per square foot, which on a typical 1,500 sq ft home adds $2,250-$3,000 to your total.

costFactors.title: Old Siding Removal
costFactors.description: If your existing siding needs to be stripped before new installation, expect to add $0.50-$1.50 per square foot for removal and disposal. Homes with multiple layers of old siding or those with lead paint (pre-1978 homes) can push removal costs to $2-$3 per square foot due to hazmat handling requirements.

costFactors.title: House Size and Stories
costFactors.description: A single-story ranch with 1,200 sq ft of siding is far cheaper to side than a two-story colonial with 2,500 sq ft. Second-story work requires scaffolding ($500-$1,500 rental), and labor rates increase 15-25% for elevated work. Complex rooflines with lots of gables and dormers add even more because each angle requires custom-cut pieces.

costFactors.title: Trim, Soffit, and Fascia Work
costFactors.description: Most siding projects also include replacing or wrapping trim, soffits, and fascia in matching aluminum or vinyl. Skipping this work saves money but leaves your home looking mismatched. Budget $1,500-$2,500 for basic aluminum wrapping and $3,000-$4,000 if you need structural soffit and fascia replacement.

costFactors.title: Insulation and House Wrap
costFactors.description: Adding fanfold insulation board or replacing old house wrap (like Tyvek) before new siding installation adds $0.50-$1.50 per square foot but can improve your home's energy efficiency by 10-20%. Insulated-back vinyl panels cost more upfront but eliminate this as a separate line item.

materialOptions.pros: Lowest cost, wide availability, easy to replace individual panels
materialOptions.cons: Fades faster, more brittle in cold weather, looks flat and plastic
materialOptions.bestFor: Rental properties, tight budgets, homes you plan to sell soon

materialOptions.pros: Good durability, better color retention, more realistic wood-grain texture
materialOptions.cons: Still less durable than fiber cement or wood, limited architectural styles
materialOptions.bestFor: Most homeowners looking for a solid balance of cost and quality

materialOptions.pros: Built-in insulation, excellent impact resistance, deep wood-grain textures, quieter home
materialOptions.cons: Higher upfront cost, fewer contractors experienced with installation
materialOptions.bestFor: Long-term homeowners in harsh climates wanting maximum energy savings

materialOptions.pros: Mimics cedar shake appearance, no maintenance, good curb appeal
materialOptions.cons: Most expensive vinyl option, limited to accent areas on many homes
materialOptions.bestFor: Craftsman or Cape Cod style homes where shake accents add character

materialOptions.pros: Modern farmhouse look, vertical lines make homes appear taller, low maintenance
materialOptions.cons: More complex installation, not ideal for homes with lots of windows breaking up wall space
materialOptions.bestFor: Modern farmhouse, contemporary, or transitional style homes

northeast.label: Northeast
northeast.note: High labor rates and shorter installation seasons push costs up. CT, MA, and NY metro areas are at the top of this range.

westCoast.label: West Coast
westCoast.note: Vinyl siding is less common in CA and the Pacific Northwest, where fiber cement dominates. Fewer vinyl specialists means higher labor rates.

southeast.label: Southeast
southeast.note: Lower labor costs and year-round installation weather keep prices down. FL and GA are among the most affordable markets for siding work.

midwest.label: Midwest
midwest.note: Vinyl siding is extremely popular in the Midwest, so competition keeps prices reasonable. OH, IN, and MI tend to be on the lower end.

mountainWest.label: Mountain West
mountainWest.note: Growing populations in CO and UT have increased contractor demand. Altitude and weather can limit installation windows.

diyVsPro.diyFeasibility: Moderate - Vinyl siding installation is one of the more DIY-friendly exterior projects, but it requires patience, proper tools, and comfort working on ladders or scaffolding.
diyTasks: Installing house wrap on single-story sections
Hanging siding panels on ground-level walls
proOnlyTasks: Second-story and gable-end installation requiring scaffolding
Repairing rotted sheathing or structural issues
Proper flashing installation around windows and doors
Lead paint abatement on pre-1978 homes
diyVsPro.diyRisks: Improper installation can void manufacturer warranties (most require certified installation for warranty coverage). Incorrect nailing - vinyl must hang loosely to expand and contract - causes buckling and cracking. Water intrusion from poor flashing around windows is the biggest long-term risk.

savingsTips: Get quotes in late fall or winter when siding contractors have slower schedules and may discount 10-15%
Choose standard horizontal lap siding instead of shake, scallop, or board-and-batten profiles to save $2-$4 per square foot
Remove old siding yourself to save $1,000-$2,000 in labor costs - just watch for lead paint on pre-1978 homes
Buy siding during spring sales at big-box stores and have your contractor install materials you provide (some will, some won't)
Skip the premium color options - standard white and light colors are cheapest and fade less than dark tones
If only one or two sides of your house face the street, consider using premium siding on visible sides and economy grade on the back
Bundle your siding project with gutter replacement to negotiate a package discount of 5-10%
Ask about manufacturer rebates - brands like CertainTeed and Alside run seasonal promotions

contractorQuestions.question: Will you remove the old siding or install over it?
contractorQuestions.why: Installing over existing siding is cheaper but hides potential sheathing damage and can create moisture problems. Removal costs more but lets you inspect and repair the wall underneath.

contractorQuestions.question: What brand and thickness of vinyl do you recommend, and why?
contractorQuestions.why: A good contractor will explain the trade-offs between .040, .044, and .046 thickness panels. If they only push the cheapest option or can't name the brand, that's a red flag.

contractorQuestions.question: How do you handle flashing around windows, doors, and penetrations?
contractorQuestions.why: Water intrusion is the number one cause of siding failure. Proper step flashing and integrated head flashing around every opening is non-negotiable. Ask to see examples from past jobs.

contractorQuestions.question: Does your installation qualify for the manufacturer's warranty?
contractorQuestions.why: Many premium vinyl siding manufacturers require certified installers for their lifetime warranties. An uncertified installer might leave you with only a basic prorated warranty.

contractorQuestions.question: Will you replace the house wrap, and is it included in the quote?
contractorQuestions.why: Old or damaged house wrap should be replaced during re-siding. Some contractors skip this step to keep bids low, but it can lead to moisture damage behind the new siding.

contractorQuestions.question: What is included in trim work - just wrapping, or full replacement?
contractorQuestions.why: Aluminum trim wrapping covers existing wood trim for a clean look. Full replacement costs more but addresses rot. Make sure the quote specifies which approach is being used.

contractorQuestions.question: How do you handle expansion and contraction with vinyl?
contractorQuestions.why: Vinyl expands significantly with temperature changes. Panels must be hung loosely with nails centered in slots, not driven tight. A contractor who doesn't mention this may not install it correctly.

secondaryKeywords: cost to replace siding on house
vinyl siding installation cost per square foot
how much does new siding cost
seo.metaDescription: Vinyl siding replacement costs $5,000-$25,000 in 2026, with most homeowners paying $7,000-$18,000. See cost breakdowns by grade, regional pricing, and use our free calculator.
